why_plausible: Script has no --help/-h handling at all (only --json/--verbose/--quick/--repair/--dry-run/--force); an agent naturally tries --help first to discover usage, but it falls into the catch-all 'Unknown option' branch and exits 1.
Transposed-letter typo of --json, a common fat-finger; hits 'Unknown option: --jsno' and exits 1.
Agent guesses a generic --format json flag (a common CLI convention) but this script only supports the bare --json flag; unrecognized, exits 1.
Agent mistakenly treats 'health' as a subcommand-style argument (echoing the script's own name); hits the catch-all unknown-option branch and exits 1.
Plausible guess at a shorthand for --from-tag; not defined, falls into the default case 'ERROR: Unknown argument' and exits 2.
Bare 'help' (no dashes), by analogy with subcommand-style CLIs; only --help/-h are parsed, so this hits the default unknown-argument case and exits 2.
Agent drops the 'from-' prefix by analogy to a simpler flag name; only --from-changelog is recognized, so this is unknown and exits 2.
Since the script's output is already JSON, an agent may add --json thinking it forces that format (a common convention elsewhere in the repo); not a recognized flag here, exits 2.
Typo of --json; the option loop's '-*)' catch-all reports 'Unknown option: --jsno' and exits 1.
Agent forgets the required <id> positional after --full; obs_id resolves empty and show_full's own check reports 'Error: Observation ID required' and exits 1.
Single-dash long-form confusion; '-help' matches the generic '-*)' catch-all rather than the '-h|--help' case, producing 'Unknown option: -help' and exiting 1.
Plausible nonexistent flag borrowed from sibling scripts (e.g. beads-health.sh has --verbose); memory-query.sh has no such flag and exits 1 via the catch-all.
Both --file and --gate are required; a bare invocation (expecting sane defaults like other read-only checkers) trips 'Error: --file and --gate are required' and exits 2.
Agent supplies only --file, forgetting the also-required --gate flag; hits the same required-args check and exits 2.
Typo'd gate value ('audti' instead of 'audit') passes the presence check but fails the enum check ('--gate must be review or audit') and exits 2.
Typo'd --file as --fiel; falls into the default 'Unknown option' case and exits 2 before the required-args check is even reached.
A subcommand is required ($# -lt 1 check); a bare invocation exits 1 via _usage.
Typo'd subcommand 'reslove' (should be 'resolve'); hits the default '*)' case 'Unknown subcommand: reslove' and exits 1.
'resolve' requires a <query> argument; omitting it trips 'resolve requires a <query> argument' and exits 1.
Bare 'help' (no dashes) by analogy with other CLIs; only '-h|--help' is parsed as a subcommand alias, so 'help' falls into 'Unknown subcommand: help' and exits 1.
'compose' requires both <source> and <target> slugs; supplying only one trips 'compose requires <source-slug> and <target-slug>' and exits 1.
Typo of --strict; falls into the default '*)' case 'Unknown arg: --strcit' and exits 2.
Agent forgets the required NAME after --skill; the parser does SINGLE_SKILL="$2"; shift 2 with no $2 present, erroring under set -u rather than validating gracefully.
Plausible nonexistent flag mirroring --verbose in sibling scripts; not recognized here, falls into the default case and exits 2.
Bare 'help' without dashes; only '-h|--help' is parsed, so this hits the default unknown-arg case and exits 2.
Agent uses --flag=value syntax (common across many CLIs) but this parser only accepts '--skill NAME' as two separate argv entries; unrecognized, exits 2.
Only the long form --help is parsed (no -h alias); '-h' falls into the default case, prints 'Unknown option: -h', and exits via usage(2).
Common misspelling of --quiet; unrecognized, exits 2 via usage.
--verbose is a plausible but nonexistent flag (present in sibling scripts like beads-health.sh); combined with valid --strict, it still hits the unknown-option branch on --verbose and exits 2.
Typo'd --file as --fil; hits the default case immediately and exits 2 before the path argument is ever consumed.
Agent treats 'validate' as a positional subcommand (by analogy to other loa scripts), but this script takes only flags; hits the default unknown-option case and exits 2.
One of --merge-sha or --merge-msg is required; a bare invocation trips 'one of --merge-sha or --merge-msg is required' and exits 2.
Agent forgets the required <sha> value; the parser's own guard '--merge-sha requires a value' catches this cleanly and exits 2.
Typo dropping the hyphen ('--mergesha' instead of '--merge-sha'); falls into the default 'unknown argument' case and exits 2.
Agent supplies only --pr-number, forgetting that --merge-sha/--merge-msg is still required; trips the same required-arg check and exits 2.
Agent forgets the required <subject> value after --merge-msg; the parser's guard '--merge-msg requires a value' catches this and exits 2.
